verdict.label: Go bodyboarding
verdict.headline: Go bodyboarding: Belgas Early morning
verdict.reason: Best combined score from the checked sources, with no major blocker for Belgas.

best.windFit: W 5 km/h · glassy · good wind fit
best.bestWind: ideal E/ENE/ESE, usable NE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

photo.credit: Photo: Tiago Lima, CC BY 2.0, via Wikimedia Commons

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: This looks like a clean fun-sized north coast window: Surfline is showing surf height around 0.6-0.9 m, while Surf-Forecast adds 1.2 m @ 11 s of swell context. The 136 kJ and 11 s period should mean enough push for bodyboard waves without looking heavy; consistency is 59/100, workable but with some waiting. The wind state is glassy, so treat it like a premium clean-water signal. The north coast rule is active because Lagide is under 1 m, so exposed beaches can turn on while Baleal looks too small.
best.shortExplanation: 0.6-0.9 m Surfline surf · 136 kJ, just enough to be useful · 11 s period · glassy

ranked.explanation: This looks like a clean small window: Surfline is showing surf height around 0.3-0.6 m, while Surf-Forecast adds 1.2 m @ 11 s of swell context. The 202 kJ and 11 s period should mean a punchier session with more push in the sets; consistency is 61/100, workable but with some waiting. The wind state is glassy, so treat it like a premium clean-water signal. Watch: spot surf looks too small.
ranked.shortExplanation: 0.3-0.6 m Surfline surf · 202 kJ, punchy enough to matter · 11 s period · glassy · Watch: spot surf looks too small.
